name: Westall School
description: The Westall School UFO incident refers to a reported UFO sighting that allegedly occurred on April 6, 1966, at Westall High School (now Westall Secondary College) in Clayton South, Victoria, Australia. Proponents claim that over 200 students and teachers witnessed a silver, saucer-shaped object descend into a nearby paddock, known as The Grange, before ascending and disappearing. Some accounts describe the object being pursued by five unidentified aircraft. The incident has become a cornerstone of Australian UFO lore, with many believing it represents a significant, unexplained event. Investigations by UFO groups and media outlets have attempted to uncover the truth behind the event. Mainstream explanations suggest the sighting was likely a misidentified experimental aircraft, a weather balloon, or even mass hysteria. The lack of definitive photographic or video evidence, coupled with varying eyewitness accounts and the passage of time, makes verifying the claims difficult. Skeptics point to the potential for memories to become distorted over time and the influence of suggestion as contributing factors to the enduring mystery. The Australian Air Force investigated at the time and concluded there was insufficient evidence to support the UFO claims. Despite this, the Westall incident continues to fascinate and fuel debate within the UFO community.
subtitle: Australian UFO sighting and its legacy.

claims.claim: A silver, saucer-shaped UFO was sighted at Westall High School.
claims.evidence: Eyewitness accounts from students and teachers present at the school on April 6, 1966.
claims.criticism: Eyewitness accounts vary significantly, and memories can be unreliable after a long period. No definitive photographic or video evidence exists.

claims.claim: The UFO landed in The Grange, leaving a circular impression.
claims.evidence: Some witnesses reported a flattened circle of grass in the paddock after the sighting.
claims.criticism: No official documentation or scientific analysis confirms the existence or cause of this alleged circle. Other explanations for ground markings are possible.

claims.claim: Five unidentified aircraft pursued the UFO.
claims.evidence: Some eyewitnesses claim to have seen multiple aircraft chasing the object.
claims.criticism: Other witnesses did not report seeing any other aircraft, and the 'aircraft' could have been misidentified birds or other aerial phenomena. The Australian Air Force investigation found no evidence of other aircraft involved.

keyFigures.name: Shane Ryan
keyFigures.role: Ufologist and researcher who has investigated the Westall incident.

keyFigures.name: Keith Basterfield
keyFigures.role: Australian UFO researcher and skeptic who has offered alternative explanations for the event.

mythologyConnections: Some connect the event to broader themes of celestial beings or visitations found in various mythologies.
Comparisons are sometimes drawn to stories of otherworldly encounters.

timeline.event: Alleged UFO sighting at Westall High School.
Australian Air Force investigation concludes with no definitive explanation.
Numerous documentaries, articles, and investigations explore the Westall incident.

sources.author: Basterfield, Keith
sources.work: Close Encounters Down Under: Australian UFO Sightings
sources.title: Basterfield, Keith
